04619.content:
Zush is organized as a small orchestration layer over separable discovery, plugin-loading, command-merging, runtime, and service components.

Observed runtime flow:
- core.bootstrap parses mock-path, loads config, runs discovery, clears runtime globals, binds services, registers hooks, then builds the root ZushGroup.
- core.discovery coordinates env selection, cache/sentry reads, stale checks, live scanning, cached package recovery, and merged tree construction.
- pluginloader.loader executes plugin __zush__.py files and extracts an instance exposing a commands dictionary.
- core.group and utils.group convert dotted command keys into nested Click groups, inject invocation context, and dispatch before/after/error hooks.
- core.runtime and core.services manage process-local providers plus detached service lifecycle control.

0534a.summary: Custom Click group composes plugin behavior
0534a.content:
The dominant design pattern is plugin composition around a custom Click group.

Important traits:
- First-wins command merge keeps registration deterministic across multiple plugin sources.
- Hook registration is inferred from plugin instance attributes instead of requiring explicit plugin interfaces.
- ZushCtx acts as an observable invocation-scoped state container for onCtxMatch behavior.
- Runtime globals and detached services are separate concerns: globals are process-local objects, while services are persisted and lifecycle-managed.

06c50.summary: Python 3.12 uv Click pytest playground workflow
06c50.content:
Current technical setup observed during inspection:
- Python 3.12 project with a src layout.
- Packaging and task execution are centered on uv and pyproject.toml.
- Runtime dependencies are Click and PyYAML.
- Dev/test dependencies include pytest, Flask, httpx, and httpcore.
- Playground packages under playground/ exercise deep trees, hooks, and provider-plus-service flows.
- Baseline validation on 2026-04-15: uv run pytest passed with 105 tests on Windows.

153eb.summary: Five-folder source layout keeps zush concerns separated
153eb.content:
Legacy memory-bank architecture context absorbed on 2026-04-15.

Current durable structure:
- src/zush is organized around core, pluginloader, configparse, mocking, and utils.
- The package root remains thin and re-exports the bootstrap surface only.
- core owns discovery, group orchestration, runtime, services, persistence, cache, env helpers, and storage abstractions.
- pluginloader owns plugin author APIs, inspection, package loading, and runtime binding after discovery chooses a concrete package path.
- configparse owns config parsing and persistence helpers.
- mocking owns mock-path parsing and temporary storage helpers used by tests and local playground-style runs.
- utils holds delegated helper implementations so run_discovery can stay focused on orchestration rather than low-level scanning details.

Operational implication:
- Refactors should preserve these concern boundaries unless there is a clear cross-cutting reason to collapse them.

27ee8.summary: Self cron uses two-phase registry model
27ee8.content:
Self cron now separates reusable command registrations from schedule entries.
- `self cron register <name> <command_path> [args/kwargs] [-d|--detach]` stores the command path, positional args, keyword args, and detach mode under `registrations`.
- `self cron add <name> <cronstring>` appends a job under `jobs` that references the registration via `target`, allowing multiple schedules to reuse the same command payload.
- Detached execution is resolved from the registration, so every schedule using that target inherits the same execution mode.
- Legacy inline job payloads remain executable so existing `cron.json` files do not break immediately.

286f8.content:
Self cron now supports delayed lifejobs attached to scheduled jobs.
- `cron.json` includes a top-level `lifejobs` map beside `registrations` and `jobs`.
- `self cron add <registration> --lifejob <target_job> --delay <seconds>` creates a delayed follower that reuses an existing registration.
- When the target job runs, attached lifejobs are rescheduled from that latest run time, so a newer parent run replaces an older pending cleanup window.
- `self cron remove <job_name>` cascades and deletes any attached lifejobs for that job.
- Lifejobs inherit detached or attached execution from their own registration target.

29e77.summary: Self cron start supports simulated time controls
29e77.content:
Self cron start now supports simulated time controls through a dedicated runtime helper layer.
- `self cron start --scale <float>` advances simulated scheduler time faster or slower than wall clock.
- `self cron start --mocktime <iso-datetime>` starts the scheduler from a fixed simulated timestamp.
- `self cron start --dry-run` evaluates jobs and lifejobs in memory, emits dispatch lines, and leaves `cron.json` unchanged.
- `src/zush/core/cron_runtime.py` owns clock advancement, mocktime parsing, and bounded test-loop execution so integration tests can simulate complex interactions without sleeping or mutating persisted state.

2b90c.summary: Self cron supports single-day completion guards
2b90c.content:
Self cron entries can opt into single-day completion guards backed by a daily JSONL ledger.
- `self cron add ... -sdc/--single-day-complete` stores the opt-in flag on individual jobs or lifejobs instead of on shared registrations.
- `src/zush/core/cron_completion.py` owns `cron_completion.jsonl`, which stores one line per ISO date with the entry names that already completed that day.
- Scheduler dispatch skips any job or lifejob marked `single_day_complete` when its name already appears in that day's ledger, and successful non-dry-run execution records the completion.
- Lifejobs clear pending due times when skipped for same-day completion so stale due entries do not keep retriggering within the same day.
